Considerable discussion of recruiting future teachers of vocational.
A_results:
agricylture took place as a means of meeting the above problem, Among
procedures which were suggested were raising the academic grade requirements,
cooperating with colleges of agriculture in recruitment efforts, the development of brochures showing what graduates in agricultural education were
doing, asking each teacher to have one graduate of his Vo-Ag department
enrolled in agricultural education, Meetings with guidance counselors, the
use of Alpha Tau Alpha members and college teams to meet with school
administrators and students were discussed as possibilities,
Sources of funds for graduate assistuntships were then discussed, Iowa
and Wisconsin reported securing funds frem their agricultural experiment
stations, Ohio and Nebraska reported limited help for teachers from commercial
organizations, It was also reported that Farmers Union Elevators have provided
funds for short-time scholarships for teachers of vocational agriculture in
their territory and that the Sears Foundation has given Limited assistance,
